notes: Primary user account (mutykovaelena@gmail.com) was locked out during SEC-AUTH-003 testing. Switched to secondary user (maseweinnouwe-5837@yopmail.com / Alice May / BMW-Group) for PCI compliance tests. Session expiry tests (3) skipped due to complexity requiring manual token manipulation. CVV masking test marked ambiguous -- CVV not masked but within CyberSource PCI-compliant iframe.

results.title: Card Number Iframe Isolation
results.notes: Card number field hosted in iframe from testflex.cybersource.com. Cross-origin access returns null for contentDocument, confirming isolation. Microform v2.0.2.

results.title: CVV Iframe Isolation
results.notes: Security code field in separate iframe from testflex.cybersource.com with fieldType=securityCode. Cross-origin access blocked.

results.title: CVV Visual Masking
results.notes: CVV digits displayed as plain text '838' rather than masked bullets. However, this is within CyberSource's PCI-compliant iframe where data never reaches the storefront domain. Masking is a CyberSource Microform configuration option, not a platform bug. Flagged for qa-lead review.

results.title: No Card Data in Network Requests
results.notes: After filling card number 4622943127013705 and CVV 838, no raw card data found in any network request to storefront domain. All requests were GraphQL cart mutations and GA4 tracking. Card data stays within CyberSource iframe domain.

results.title: No Card Data in Browser Storage
results.notes: Checked localStorage, sessionStorage, and cookies. No card numbers or CVV values stored anywhere. Only safe tokens and cart metadata persisted.

results.title: HTTPS Enforcement
results.notes: All connections use HTTPS. Storefront URL is https://. CyberSource iframe sources use https://testflex.cybersource.com. No mixed content detected. Scanned all resource URLs and found no http:// resources.

results.title: JWT Token Authentication
results.notes: Both iframes contain JWT tokens in URL with valid three-part structure (header.payload.signature). JWT includes iss='Flex API', iat/exp timestamps, allowed card networks (VISA, MASTERCARD, AMEX, DISCOVER, etc.), and targetOrigins restricted to the storefront domain.

results.title: Session Token Security
results.notes: Access token stored in localStorage as JWT with exp claim. Token includes user ID, store context, and permissions. Token refresh mechanism works via /connect/token endpoint.

results.title: Logout Token Revocation
results.notes: Logout clears localStorage tokens and redirects to unauthenticated state. Sign in link reappears in header, cart badge cleared.

results.title: Account Lockout Protection
results.notes: Account locked after 5 failed login attempts with clear message: 'Your account has been temporarily locked. Please try again later.' Correct password also rejected while locked. Lockout duration appears to be >5 minutes (possibly resets on each failed attempt during lockout).

results.title: XSS Prevention - Search
results.notes: Entered <script>alert('XSS')</script> in search. Script not executed. Input displayed as escaped text in search results. No alert triggered.

results.title: XSS Prevention - Forms
results.notes: Entered XSS payload in profile name field. Payload saved and rendered as escaped text. No script execution. Original name restored after test.

results.title: SQL Injection Prevention
results.notes: Entered " OR '1'='1 in search. Query handled safely with no SQL errors. Returned normal empty results. Backend uses Elasticsearch, not direct SQL queries from search input.

results.title: Path Traversal Prevention
results.notes: Attempted ../../../etc/passwd in URL paths. Server returned 404 or redirected to home page. No sensitive file access. Path traversal blocked.

results.title: CSRF Token Validation
results.notes: SPA uses JWT Bearer tokens in Authorization header for all API requests (not traditional form-based CSRF tokens). This is the standard CSRF mitigation pattern for SPAs -- Bearer tokens are not automatically sent by browsers like cookies, making traditional CSRF attacks ineffective.

results.title: CORS Configuration
results.notes: API requests from storefront origin succeed. CORS headers properly configured to allow the storefront origin. No cross-origin errors in console for legitimate API calls.

results.title: No Credentials in Logs
results.notes: Checked browser console after login, cart operations, and payment form interaction. No passwords, card numbers, or sensitive tokens logged. Only standard Vue/Apollo dev tools messages and expected GA4 tracking.

results.title: Error Message Safety
results.notes: Triggered authentication errors (wrong password, locked account). Error messages are generic and safe: 'Login is failed' and 'Your account has been temporarily locked'. No stack traces, internal paths, or system details exposed.

results.title: Session expiry during checkout - re-auth preserves cart
results.notes: Requires manual token invalidation and waiting for token expiry. Complex test requiring dedicated session with controlled token lifecycle. Recommend manual testing or API-driven token revocation approach.

results.title: Session re-auth during checkout - resume from last step
results.notes: Requires controlled token expiry during active checkout. Depends on SEC-SESSION-001 methodology. Recommend manual testing with DevTools token manipulation.

results.title: Session expiry does not create partial or orphan order
results.notes: Requires forcing token expiry at exact moment of order submission. Critical safety test but requires precise timing control. Recommend dedicated manual testing session with xAPI order verification.
